revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大六天 (飯能市)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Dairokuten Shrine | image = File:Dairokuten jinjya (Hanno, Saitama) 02.JPG | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hannō|en|Hannō|qid=Q850472|lt=Hannō|1=Hannō}}, Oaza Hannō | coordinates = {{Coord|35.85705|N|139.3056722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Omotaru-no-Mikoto|ja|面足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}}, {{ill|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|ja|惶根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}}, {{ill|Taishakuten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishakuten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | status = Not a registered religious corporation | founded = Unknown | other_names = Dairokuten, Dairokuten-sama, {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}}|deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qid=Q11152535|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}; {{ill|Paranimmitavasavatti|qid=Q13578873|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven}} | location = {{ill|Hannō|qid=Q850472|1=Hannō}} }} '''Dairokuten Shrine''' (大六天) is a small {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|小祠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祠|1=Hokora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1442984}} located in {{ill|Hannō|en|Hannō|qid=Q850472|lt=Hannō|1=Hannō}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is not registered as a religious corporation. To its west lies a city-designated cultural property, the "Hongo Dairokuten Aoi Stone Stupa and Oak Tree." == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown. In {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}, "Dairokuten" refers to {{ill|Omotaru-no-Mikoto|ja|面足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}} and {{ill|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|ja|惶根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}}, while in {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, it is associated with {{ill|Taishakuten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishakuten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the {{ill|Dairokuten Maō|ja|第六天魔王|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven (Dairokuten Maō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Maō|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔}}. It is believed that the spread of worship for this deity was influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, due to its proximity to sites like {{ill|Sasai Kannon-dō|ja|笹井観音堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasai Kannon-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oegoyama Main Temple|ja|越生山本坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oegoyama Main Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine likely dates back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} or {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. The shrine area, named "Dairokuten," suggests long-standing local reverence. The current shrine building was reconstructed during the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}. The shrine also features an {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|qid=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} depicting a "facing tengu," which is included in works by folklorist {{ill|Hiroshi Iwai|ja|岩井宏實|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshi Iwai|1=Hiromi Iwai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11474045}}. == Hongo Dairokuten Aoi Stone Stupa and Oak Tree == Behind the shrine stands a large {{ill|oak tree|ja|樫樹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oak tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stone stupas, designated as a municipal cultural property of Hannō in {{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}}. The oak tree, a {{ill|Quercus myrsinifolia|en|Quercus myrsinifolia|qid=Q1075985|lt=Shirakashi|1=Quercus myrsinifolia}}, has a hollow caused by lightning but remains alive. Two stone stupas are embedded in its roots, believed to date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. === "Legendary Grave of Hatakeyama Shigetada" === Local legend claims one of the stone stupas is the grave of {{ill|Hatakeyama Shigetada|en|Hatakeyama Shigetada|qid=Q3128268|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}}, a samurai who died in {{ill|1205|en|1205|qid=Q5229|lt=1205|1=1205}}. According to the tale, his retainers attempted to transport his remains to {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|1=Q108779097|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108779097}}, but the carriage became immovable at this site, which was interpreted as Shigetada's spirit wishing to rest there. Thus, he was buried at the location, later named "Mikaeri-zaka" ("Look-back Slope"). However, historical accounts indicate his remains were interred in {{ill|Futamatagawa|ja|二俣川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futamatagawa|1=Futamatagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371340}}, and another grave exists in {{ill|Fukaya, Saitama|en|Fukaya, Saitama|qid=Q734532|lt=Fukaya|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, Saitama, his birthplace. <gallery widths="210px" heights="210px"> File:Dairokuten jinjya (Hanno, Saitama) 04.JPG|Hollowed Shirakashi tree File:Dairokuten jinjya (Hanno, Saitama) 06.JPG|Two stone stupas embedded in roots File:Dairokuten jinjya (Hanno, Saitama) 05b.jpg|Sign stating "Legendary Grave of Hatakeyama Shigetada" </gallery> == location and Access == * Address: 320-1 Oaza Hannō, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture * {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|qid=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}} "Hannō Station" – 20-minute walk * Accessible via {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11422566}}, alight at "Iwane-bashi" bus stop (3-minute walk) == Related Pages == * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} – Similar shrines exist across {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, sometimes written as "大六天." {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{Dairokuten Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|大六天 (飯能市)|version=101524515|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Dairokuten-jinja (Hanno)}} {{wikidata link|Q11602400}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Dairokuten Shrines]] [[Category:History of Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:History of Japan]] [[Category:Religious studies]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
